CREATE TABLE test_dates (dt DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2020-01-01');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2020-03-22');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2021-12-25');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2020-02-29');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2020-12-31');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2021-01-01');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2019-12-31');
SELECT week(dt) FROM test_dates WHERE dt = DATE '2020-01-01';
SELECT week(dt) FROM test_dates WHERE dt BETWEEN DATE '2020-01-01' AND DATE '2020-12-31';
SELECT COUNT(*), week(dt) FROM test_dates GROUP BY week(dt);
SELECT week(dt) + 1 as next_week FROM test_dates WHERE dt < DATE '2020-03-22';
SELECT dt, week(dt) FROM test_dates ORDER BY dt DESC;
CREATE TABLE other_dates (id INT, dt TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO other_dates VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2020-03-22 10:00:00');
INSERT INTO other_dates VALUES (2, TIMESTAMP '2021-07-18 14:30:00');
SELECT od.id, week(od.dt) FROM other_dates od JOIN test_dates td ON week(od.dt) = week(td.dt);
SELECT week(dt) AS week_number FROM (SELECT dt FROM test_dates WHERE dt IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT week(CAST(NOW() AS DATE)) AS current_week;
SELECT week(dt) FROM test_dates WHERE week(dt) = (SELECT MAX(week(dt)) FROM test_dates);
WITH week_data AS (SELECT week(dt) AS week_num FROM test_dates) SELECT week_num, COUNT(*) FROM week_data GROUP BY week_num;
